{{Quote|It used to take two years and 14 filings to register a new currency. We're pleased to have reduced the process to under 30 minutes, with a basic threshold and exclusivity agreement.|Lo Vapeets|<ref name="HoloNetNews" />}}
'''Lo Vapeet''', a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], was the [[Vice-Chair of Communications]] for the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] during the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="HoloNetNews" />

==Biography==
{{Quote|For our customers choosing to strike out on their own, continuing to trade in Republic credits is not in line with their independent spirit.|Lo Vapeets|<ref name="HoloNetNews" />}}
Lo Vapeet was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] staunchly loyal to the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]], and often spoke in favor of the company's operations on [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] and in [[Shadowfeed/Legends|CIS Shadowfeed]] feeds prior to and during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

During the [[Separatist Crisis/Legends|Separatist Crisis]], when the Banking Clan developed new [[Currency/Legends|currencies]] for [[Star system/Legends|systems]] leaving the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], it reduced the [[Time/Legends|time]] to register a new currency to just 30 [[Standard minute/Legends|standard minutes]]—it had previously taken two [[Standard year/Legends|years]] and 14 filings to register a new currency.

Vice-Chair Vapeet denied reports of his organization sending armed [[IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tank/Legends|IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tanks]] to [[Praesitlyn/Legends|Praesitlyn]], claiming that they were equipment stolen from the Banking Clan's ledgers by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] rather than proof of any partisanshrip with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].

The Clone Wars soon ensued, with the Banking Clan providing substantial support to the Confederacy in spite of the non-exclusive arrangement. Still, the Vice-Chair of Communications continued to defend the company by iterating that it was only acting in its best interests in its assistance to the Separatist Alliance.
